instruction: Your story has a few continuity errors and you're a little inconsistent. The man never said "I told you already, we just sell lemonade! And if you come back again, I’ll put you in the blender!" as that would be too violent.
y_star:
You're absolutely correct, and I appreciate you pointing that out. My previous retelling had some inconsistencies, and I apologize for that. Here's an accurate and consistent version of the story from "The Duck Song" by Bryant Oden:

Once upon a time, there was a little duck who waddled up to a lemonade stand and asked the man, "Hey, got any grapes?" The man replied, "No, we just sell lemonade here. It's cold and it's refreshing, and it's all you need on a hot day." The duck sighed and walked away, disappointed.

The next day, the curious little duck returned and asked the same question, "Hey, got any grapes?" The man, now a bit more annoyed, responded, "No, we just sell lemonade. Can I get you a glass?" The duck shook its head and walked away again.

On the third day, the duck returned once more and asked, "Hey, got any grapes?" The man, growing more frustrated, said, "No, we just sell lemonade. You're asking the same thing every day. Why don't you just go find some grapes on your own?" The duck shrugged and walked away, undeterred.

The next day, the duck returned and asked, "Hey, got any grapes?" The man, now clearly exasperated, said, "No, we don't sell grapes! Why are you asking me this every day?" The duck replied, "Oh, so then... got any grapes?" The man was taken aback and simply said, "No," before the duck waddled off with a proud smile.

This version maintains the humor and absurdity of the original story while ensuring continuity and staying true to the intended tone of the song. Let me know if you'd like further clarification!
